summary: Large-scale text-mining pipeline extracting 35+ material properties from 4,000+ scientific articles to build a unified superconducting materials database supporting ML modelling and data-driven discovery.
title: Superconductor Augmented World
description:
Machine learning studies of superconductors have largely relied on composition-based descriptors, owing to the limited availability of structured experimental datasets. Despite significant progress in materials informatics, no unified superconducting-materials database yet integrates experimental properties, theoretical descriptors, and crystal structure information.

In this project, we developed a large-scale text-mining pipeline to extract more than 35 material properties from over 4,000 published scientific articles. The resulting dataset is designed to support improved machine learning models, structure–property analysis, and data-driven superconductor discovery, by combining natural language processing, scientific document understanding, and materials informatics workflows.
